title: exocarpos lauterbachianus
intro: Exocarpos lauterbachianus, also known as the Lauterbach's quandong, is a species of flowering plant in the family Santalaceae. It is a small shrub native to Australia, where its fruits have been used for centuries by indigenous communities for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Exocarpos lauterbachianus include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Traditionally, the plant's fruits are prepared as a decoction, tincture, or infused in water to make a tea, and its seeds are used to make an herbal powder or oil for medicinal purposes.
